prompt:
I strongly disagree with this opinion. It is my belief that young people should go in the university after school. Only after that should they go working.

First of all, university provides valuable expertise for its students. Not only does it give hard skills like STEM education or medical degree, but it also teaches people how to study, perceive and consume information in large quantities. If you are to successfully graduate, you are almost guaranteed to have good critical thinking skills. Many people nowadays lack such knowledge which poses a challenge for safety and evolution of the society.

Secondly, going to university is a good way to socialize. You are going to be put through tough challenges, and going at it alone is not easy. But there are people in your study group which you can work and create meaningful relationships with. You would think that with the rise of the Internet finding and maintaining friendships became easier, but nothing can replace real-life interactions as they usually last longer and generally are more intimate.

Last but not least, human brains are the most flexible in their early twenties - right after school. If you spend this time working instead of studying, you might lose all that potential and never achieve success later. With free education in Russia, this is the best option you have to ensure stable and prosperous life for you and your relatives.

In the end, I would like to say that given the choice again, I would still choose going to the university over anything else. This is the place I have matured in and gained good connections. And I think that other young people should do that too.

messages.content:
You are an expert English language examiner.
Evaluate the student's text based on the official CEFR scale.

## Scoring Rubric
- A1/A2 (Basic): Simple sentences, limited vocabulary. Errors are expected.
- B1/B2 (Independent): Can express complex ideas. Grammar errors are allowed if they don't block meaning.
- C1/C2 (Proficient): Flexible language use, wide vocabulary. Occasional minor slips are allowed.

## Critical Instructions
1. FOCUS ON VOCABULARY AND COMPLEXITY. Do not give a low score just because of a few grammar mistakes if the vocabulary is advanced.
2. A text with complex sentences and good idioms should be B2 or C1, even with some errors.
3. Do NOT default to A2. Look for reasons to give a HIGHER score.
4. Return result ONLY as a JSON object.
5. Optional context blocks may be present: CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, RANKING_ANCHORS.
   Use them as supporting evidence only. Do not copy labels blindly.
6. For each error object, cefr_threshold = the lowest CEFR level from which incorrect use of this element should be treated as an error in this methodology.

## 6) Output JSON (ONLY JSON)
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + key rationale)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: There is an opinion that too many young people now want to get a university degree. It would be better for society if most of them start working after finishing secondary school. How much do you agree with this point of view? State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.
STUDENT_TEXT: I strongly disagree with this opinion. It is my belief that young people should go in the university after school. Only after that should they go working.

First of all, university provides valuable expertise for its students. Not only does it give hard skills like STEM education or medical degree, but it also teaches people how to study, perceive and consume information in large quantities. If you are to successfully graduate, you are almost guaranteed to have good critical thinking skills. Many people nowadays lack such knowledge which poses a challenge for safety and evolution of the society.

Secondly, going to university is a good way to socialize. You are going to be put through tough challenges, and going at it alone is not easy. But there are people in your study group which you can work and create meaningful relationships with. You would think that with the rise of the Internet finding and maintaining friendships became easier, but nothing can replace real-life interactions as they usually last longer and generally are more intimate.

Last but not least, human brains are the most flexible in their early twenties - right after school. If you spend this time working instead of studying, you might lose all that potential and never achieve success later. With free education in Russia, this is the best option you have to ensure stable and prosperous life for you and your relatives.

In the end, I would like to say that given the choice again, I would still choose going to the university over anything else. This is the place I have matured in and gained good connections. And I think that other young people should do that too.
TEXT_TYPE: essay
